name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
resistance_profile: With dumbbells, tension is lowest at the bottom and highest near horizontal; cables load the bottom (lengthened) portion better.
rom_notes: Raise to roughly shoulder height; the very top adds little tension. Control the lowering phase.
cues: Lead with the elbows, not the hands
Slight forward lean loads the side delt more
execution_steps: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand resting at your sides.
Lean forward very slightly and keep a soft bend in both elbows.
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides, leading with your elbows, up to about shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower slowly under control.
good_when: You only have dumbbells (home or minimal-equipment gym).
You are learning the movement and want a simple, accessible starting option.
bad_when: You want strong tension in the stretched position — a cable loads the bottom range better.
You tend to swing the weight up with momentum instead of controlling it.
common_errors: Going too heavy and shortening the range
progressions: Switch to a cable for better lengthened tension
